kill_fact: As an EVENT-DRIVEN special situation the asymmetry does not survive: the labeled catalyst is a Delivery Hero event mislabeled onto the Prosus ticker, and it carries no defined-risk floor. INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED: (1) the forced selling is of DELIVERY HERO shares, not Prosus shares (EU remedy from the Aug-2025 Just Eat Takeaway clearance requires Prosus to cut its DHER stake below ~10%), so there is no supply overhang ON Prosus to clear. (2) DHER is a low-single-digit-% sliver of Prosus's ~EUR87bn mkt cap / ~EUR130bn NAV (NAV is ~80% a 22.8% Tencent stake), already cut from ~27% to ~21-22% via the Uber (EUR20) and Aspex (EUR22) tranches - so whether it is sold, kept, or waived the per-share impact on Prosus is trivial. (3) the Uber EUR33/share bid for all of Delivery Hero is confirmed INDICATIVE / non-binding (Delivery Hero 23-May-2026 release) and was rejected by DHER - it is not a signed agreement backstopping any Prosus payout. (4) the board's quoted floor 37.37 is essentially today's live price (~37.13-38.35), so event asymmetry to the floor is ~zero, and that floor is NOT a fixed deal value - it floats with Tencent / China-tech beta (uncapped, unhedged, the actual driver: the stock fell ~50 to ~37 on Tencent, not food delivery). No dated, binding forcing function exists for a PROSUS re-rate. This is a Tencent-discount value play wearing a borrowed catalyst label; it must not be sized as a defined-risk event arbitrage.
corrections: (1) The EU did NOT waive/drop the divestiture remedy - it GRANTED an EXTENSION to 11 Oct 2026 (Bloomberg/Reuters 2-Jun-2026, at Naspers's request); Prosus only ASKED the EU to end the forced sale (Bloomberg 25-May-2026) - a request, not a grant. Any reading that the remedy is gone is wrong; the must-sell obligation still stands, merely relaxed to Oct 11. (2) Live price is stale in the card (39.30); verified ~37.13-38.35 (mid/late-Jun 2026), which sits AT the quoted floor 37.37 - confirming the event asymmetry has effectively collapsed at the current trade. (3) The genuine merit is a VALUE thesis (deep ~30-35% NAV discount, net-cash holdco, open-ended buyback ~$42bn returned, +18% NAV/shr accretion) - real, but it is discount-convergence gated on Tencent, NOT an expectations-arbitrage on a dated event.
evidence: Delivery Hero release via CNBC/Reuters, 23 May 2026 ('Delivery Hero confirms takeover offer from Uber'): Uber's EUR33/share proposal is INDICATIVE (non-binding), to all shareholders, ~$11bn, BaFin/antitrust pending - subsequently rejected by DHER; the residual-stake mark rests on an unsigned bid, not a deal floor.

Bloomberg 'Prosus Gets Until Oct. 11 for Delivery Hero Sale After Uber Bid' + Reuters/TradingView (2 Jun 2026): EU EXTENDED the deadline to 11 Oct 2026 at Naspers's request; the only hard date forces DHER selling, not a Prosus re-rate, and the divestiture obligation (cut below ~10%) remains in force.

Prosus disposals (billionaires.africa 17-Apr & 13-May-2026; Prosus IR): 4.5% of DHER sold to Uber at EUR20 (~EUR270m) and ~5% to Aspex at EUR22 (~EUR335m), cutting the stake from ~27% toward ~21-22% - premium tranches, ~two-thirds executed, immaterial vs ~EUR87bn Prosus mkt cap.

Prosus NAV/price, mid-late Jun 2026 (stockanalysis / investing.com / Prosus IR): ~22.8% Tencent stake ≈ ~80% of NAV; stock ~EUR37.1-38.4 at ~35% discount to NAV, live (~37.13) sitting AT the board floor 37.37 - downside floats with Tencent/China beta, not bounded by any deal value.
